exemplar.title: To what extent can the prevalence of child trafficking in South Africa be attributed to their government's ineffective National Development Plansâ anti-poverty policies and implementation?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent can the prevalence of child trafficking in South Africa be attributed to their government's ineffective National Development Plansâ anti-poverty policies and implementation?

strengths: Clear and focused introduction defining child trafficking and poverty with contextual statistics
Explicit, sharply focused research question linked throughout the discussion
Comprehensive methodology with rationale for source selection and triangulation
weaknesses: Occasional repetition and minor typographical errors reduce precision
Some redundancy in restating the research question within the aims section
Limited range of primary sources (only one interview) and peer-reviewed academic work
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student communicates the topic accurately and effectively, framing the investigation with comprehensive explanatory detail and a sharply focused research question. The methodology is well-rationalized, showing informed selection of sources. Minor lapsesâredundant phrasing, small typos and a single primary sourceâprevent the award of full marks, placing the work solidly in the 5â6 band at a score of 5.

strengths: Consistent, purposeful integration of up-to-date source material that reinforces analytical points
Coherent and accurate knowledge of Global Politics concepts (e.g., Marxism, intersectionality, governance theory)
Accurate and consistent use of key terminology with clear concept definitions
weaknesses: Some reliance on news outlets limits the depth of scholarly engagement
Occasional stylistic slips (e.g., minor typos) that do not impede meaning but affect polish
A few policy recommendations lack corresponding UN conventions, reducing completeness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates clear and coherent knowledge of the topic, applying appropriate source material and consistently using disciplinary terminology. While the reliance on journalistic sources and minor errors limit the depth of academic rigour, the overall performance merits a high band score of 5.

strengths: Breadth and currency of research consistently relevant to the research question
Excellent analysis supported by statistics, theory and vivid interview anecdotes
Focused argumentation with a well-developed counter-argument and strong rebuttal
Concrete, actionable policy recommendations linked to human-rights frameworks
Clear critical evaluation of source limitations and implementation challenges
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on NGO and news reports rather than peer-reviewed literature
Evaluation of biases remains mostly descriptive rather than deeply analytical
Some causal leaps (e.g., from unemployment figures to trafficking vulnerability) could be further substantiated with quantitative data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation presents an excellent line of argument, underpinned by relevant and varied research, effective analysis and robust critical evaluation. The inclusion of counter-arguments, comprehensive policy recommendations and reflective commentary on methodology demonstrates sophisticated critical thinking. Minor methodological and source-selection limitations do not detract from the overall excellence of the work, warranting the maximum score of 12.

strengths: Logical, clear structure with comprehensive table of contents and well-oriented headings
Consistent, correctly applied layout elements (title, word count, citations, bibliography)
Smooth transitions between sections that support reader comprehension
weaknesses: Long paragraphs and large tables occasionally reduce readability
Minor inconsistencies in heading hierarchy (â<h2>â vs â<h3>â)
Table width issues that could be improved by splitting or sub-heading segments
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs structure and layout meet the highest standards for clarity and coherence. All required elements (title, word count, page numbering, citations) are present and correctly formatted. While a few formatting refinements could enhance readability, the presentation fully satisfies the top-band criteria, earning 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The student advances a logical argument: systemic poverty (supported by statistics, theory and interview evidence) leads to vulnerability, the NDPâs shortcomings perpetuate poverty, hence trafficking persists. The essay contrasts stated NDP goals with unemployment data, applies Marxist and intersectional lenses, and anticipates a regional counter-argument.  

Conclusions are generally well supported, though some leaps (e.g. causal weight of unemployment figures) lack deeper empirical substantiation, and evaluation of sources remains limited. Consequently the analysis fits solidly in the 7â9 band but not higher than 7.

Research draws on government statistics, NGO reports, an interview and international documents, showing breadth and mostly strong relevance to the research question. Data are current (2021â23) and tied to analysis of the NDP and poverty.  

The absence of more peer-reviewed academic literature and heavy dependence on journalistic pieces restrain excellence, but overall the research is âgoodâ and mostly pertinent, meriting the 7â9 band at its lower end.

The essay follows a logical structure: introduction, thematic chapters, counter-argument, recommendations, conclusion, bibliography and appendix. Headings, sub-headings and a detailed table of contents orient the reader effectively.  

Transitions are generally clear and each section advances the argument, supporting understanding. Minor redundancies and lengthy tables slightly impede flow, but overall the structure is clear and appropriate; hence the top band with full 4 marks.

The essay demonstrates coherent knowledge of Global Politics, integrating concepts such as Marxism, intersectionality, universalism and governance theory. Historical context (apartheid) and contemporary policy details (NDP targets) are accurately summarised and linked to trafficking dynamics.  

Use of evidence is generally effective, though deeper engagement with academic debates on development policy could enhance sophistication.  

Overall clarity and coherence place it firmly in the top band; minor gaps justify a score of 5 rather than 6.

Discussion includes a clear line of reasoning, a counter-argument on regional trafficking networks and a rebuttal connecting interdependence to policy responsibility. Policy recommendations are aligned with human-rights instruments and contain implementation guidance, showing evaluative thinking.  

Critical evaluation is present but partial: effectiveness of proposed laws is asserted more than weighed, and source limitations are noted rather than explored. This represents âgoodâ focused argument with partial critical evaluation, justifying a 7.

Formal presentation elementsâtitle, word count (3 959), page-style section dividers, superscript referencing, bibliography and appendixâare all present. A consistent layout with headings, tables and citation superscripts supports readability.  

While page numbers are not visible in the extract and minor formatting errors (mis-typed heading, table width issues) occur, the overall layout meets IB requirements and aids comprehension, warranting the upper-band score of 4.

Key terminologyâ"interdependence", "human rights", "inequality", "governance theory", "Marxist analysis"âis used accurately and consistently. Definitions are provided where necessary, and concepts are applied rather than merely named.  

Occasional stylistic slips (e.g. âCounterarargumentâ) do not impede understanding. The sustained, correct employment of Global Politics vocabulary supports the upper-band award; a 5 reflects these minor errors.

The student presents the topic of child trafficking and poverty in South Africa in a clear, focused manner from the outset. The introduction gives relevant definitions, contextual statistics and an explicit statement of significance, while later sections return to the same framing.  

Explanatory detail is comprehensive: the reader is told why poverty matters, why the National Development Plan (NDP) is central, and how the issue links to key Global Politics concepts (human rights, inequality, interdependence).  

Minor lapses (e.g. occasional repetition, small typographical errors) limit absolute precision, but the topic is nevertheless communicated accurately and effectively; hence the upper-band award but not full marks.

The methodology section identifies primary (interview) and a varied set of secondary sources (government data, NGO reports, news media) and comments on credibility, bias and limitations. The student explains the rationale for selecting Ms Moosajee and how gaps in academic literature were mitigated.  

Source range is appropriate to Global Politics and clearly connected to the question. Evidence of informed and purposeful selection (e.g. preference for up-to-date material, triangulation of statistics) is shown.  

Minor shortcomingsâlimited peer-reviewed academic work and only a single primary sourceâprevent full marks, so 5 is awarded.

The research question is explicit, specific and sharply focused on one causal pathway: whether ineffective anti-poverty elements of the NDP explain the prevalence of child trafficking. Throughout the essay the argument repeatedly returns to this focus (e.g. analysis of unemployment targets, critique of short-term measures, counter-argument section).  

The wording is slightly cumbersome and could have been shortened, but it still provides clear guidance for the subsequent discussion, showing a strong connection between question and content.  

Overall this fits solidly within the 5â6 band; a mark of 5 reflects the slight room for tighter phrasing.

Citations are woven into the argument, backing claims about trafficking prevalence, unemployment figures and policy content. The primary interview is quoted directly and contextualised.  

Most sources are appropriate and up-to-date; the student frequently links data to analytical points rather than simply listing it. Some reliance on news outlets limits academic rigour, but application remains clearly relevant.  

Given the consistent, purposeful use of material, the work merits the 5â6 band; a 5 reflects the uneven depth of engagement with scholarly publications.

annotations.comment: The topic and research question are clearly presented at the outset, giving the reader immediate clarity on the focus of the investigation.
The research question wording is slightly cumbersome; consider tightening to improve readability and precision (e.g., shorten phrasing while retaining scope).
The table of contents is comprehensive and well-structured, orienting the reader effectively through clear headings and page references.
Consider adding a brief preface or summary before the table of contents to improve flow from the title page.
The introduction effectively defines child trafficking and links it to poverty, demonstrating clear contextual understanding.
The aims section repeats the research question mid-paragraph; consolidate this repetition to maintain focus and avoid redundancy.
The methodology articulates a clear rationale for source selection and triangulation, demonstrating purposeful planning.
The methodology relies heavily on news and NGO reports; consider incorporating more peer-reviewed academic articles to enhance scholarly rigour.
The description of source limitations is reflective but remains descriptive; deepen the critique by assessing specific bias impacts on argument.
The narrative describing the primary interview source clarifies its relevance, strengthening the methodologyâs credibility.
The reliance on anecdotal interview content could be balanced with quantitative data to avoid overgeneralization.
Statistical figures from credible institutions are integrated effectively, reinforcing the link between poverty and trafficking.
The causal connection between unemployment percentages and trafficking vulnerability is asserted; consider adding correlative or regression evidence to support this leap.
The interview anecdote vividly illustrates trafficking mechanics, enriching the analysis with on-the-ground perspective.
Long paragraphs reduce readability; break this account into smaller thematic units for clarity.
The use of Marxist theory to contextualize systemic inequality demonstrates sophisticated disciplinary knowledge.
Percentage data from the World Bank would benefit from a brief note on data collection methods to enhance source critique.
The heading level varies (<h2> vs <h3>) for comparable sections; maintain consistent heading hierarchy.
The intersectionality discussion adds depth by linking race and gender, showcasing nuanced knowledge of concepts.
Support claims of gender-based discrimination with specific statistics or studies to strengthen the argument.
The NDP policy summary is concise and accurately describes goals, reflecting strong understanding of policy frameworks.
This section is descriptive; integrate critique of why some policy targets may be unrealistic or poorly designed.
The large single-table block on page 11 hampers readability; consider splitting it or adding subheadings per action category.
Critical evaluation of why these âshort-termâ measures have failed in practice would strengthen the discussion.
The counterargument section engages with interdependence theory well, showing critical awareness of regional dynamics.
Streamline this argument by directly linking each point back to how it affects the research question.
The rebuttal would benefit from specific examples of successful regional anti-poverty collaborations disrupting trafficking networks.
The linkage of UDHR Article 4 to policy recommendations demonstrates coherent application of key Global Politics concepts.
Recommendations 4 and 5 in the table lack corresponding UN conventions; complete these entries to ensure full alignment.
The policy recommendations are well-structured and actionable, linking theory to concrete measures.
The conclusion restates main findings clearly but omits reflection on research limitations and decision-making processes.
Add a reflective section evaluating methodological setbacks and how they shaped your analysis to demonstrate deeper engagement.
The conclusion summarises arguments effectively but could be enhanced by proposing directions for future research.
Word count, subject, and title are correctly included, meeting IB formatting requirements for SL investigations.
Some entries in the table of contents lack consistent capitalization; ensure uniform title case for section headings.
The visible footnote markup (<sup> tags) disrupts readability; revise formatting to proper superscript style.
The section lacks a citation date for the interview transcript; include the date and format according to IB style.
Ensure percentage formatting uses consistent mathematical notation, e.g., $$32.9\%$$ instead of legacy HTML.
